Team Captain Guide

team-captains
  1. Quick Start
  2. Home & My Projects
  3. Project Details
  4. Managing Volunteers
  5. Tasks
  6. Expenses & Reimbursements Coming Soon
  7. Photos & Notes
  8. Requesting Help & Event Association

Quick Start

As a Team Captain, you lead one or more SWAT projects from assignment through completion — coordinating with the family, recruiting and scheduling volunteers, tracking tasks and notes, and documenting the work with photos. This guide walks through every tool you'll use.

How to get there

Sign in at waterboyz.kinops.io. Team Captains see a personalized home page with My Projects, My Tasks, and a Projects entry in the top navigation.

First: find your projects

Your home page shows up to five active projects under My Projects. Click View all or the Projects nav link to see every project assigned to you in the swat-projects datastore. Open a project to land on its Instructions tab.

Second: review the project banner and details

At the top of every project page you'll see a status banner. When a project is in Planning, it's not yet visible to volunteers — head to Details to fill in the scheduled date, skills/equipment needed, and set status to Ready to Work when you're ready to recruit.

Third: recruit and prepare your team

Use Volunteers to add and manage your project team, Tasks to build a checklist with estimated man hours, and Notes to capture family communication and planning notes with version history.

Fourth: document and close out

On the day of the project, mark volunteers Present, check off tasks as they complete, and upload photos via Photos. When everything is done, update the status to Completed and fill in the completion date on Details.
Tips
  • Each tab shows a <strong>Coming Soon</strong> or lock indicator when something isn't available yet. <strong>Expenses</strong> is not wired up in the current release — document reimbursements in <strong>Notes</strong> for now.
  • Scrolling the mobile app? The project sections collapse into a single dropdown at the top of the project page. On desktop they appear as a sidebar.
  • If your project says <em>Closed</em>, it's read-only. Ask SWAT Leadership if you need it reopened.

Home & My Projects

Your home page is the fastest way to find projects and tasks that need your attention. It pulls from the <code>swat-projects</code> datastore, your nominations, and the Task API.

How to get there

Navigate to the portal root (/) after signing in, or click the Waterboyz logo in the top nav.

Home hero and greeting

The top banner greets you by name with the Project Captain eyebrow. The home layout has two columns on desktop: My Projects and My Nominations on the left, My Tasks on the right.

My Projects list

Shows up to five of your most recent active projects (status Draft or Submitted in core state), each with a status badge, project name, and city/state. Click a row to open the project, or click View all to land on the full /project-captains list with filters.

My Tasks sidebar

Lists your open approval/fulfillment tasks from the Task API. By default this is filtered to my tasks and open only — click View all to go to /actions where you can toggle between tasks assigned to you individually and tasks assigned to any of your teams.

Nominate a Family

Below My Nominations is a shortcut to submit a new swat-project-nomination or christmas-alive-family-nomination. Every Team Captain can nominate families — nominations go to SWAT Leadership for approval.

Projects listing page

The full Projects page at /project-captains shows every project assigned to you with filter controls for status, county, and search. Each row links to the project's Instructions tab.

Field reference

Project Captain
The username of the captain on a <code>swat-projects</code> record. The home page filters on this field matching your username.
Project Status
Workflow state — <em>Planning</em>, <em>Ready to Work</em>, <em>Active</em>, <em>Ongoing</em>, <em>Completed</em>, <em>Canceled</em>. Drives the status badge and banner on every project view.
Tips
  • The task count on the sidebar updates when you mark tasks complete, but you may need to refresh if you completed something in another tab.
  • If you expected a project to appear and it doesn't, confirm the Project Captain field matches your exact username — SWAT Leadership assigns this on approval.

Project Details

The Details tab is where you manage the core project record — status, scheduling, family information, skills and equipment needed, and the link to a serve day event. Changes here are saved directly to the <code>swat-projects</code> submission.

How to get there

Open a project from your home page or Projects list, then click the Details section in the sidebar (or the dropdown on mobile).

Setting project status

Status drives visibility. A Planning project is hidden from volunteers — the yellow banner at the top of the project reminds you to switch to Ready to Work when you're ready to recruit. Active and Ongoing projects are no longer listed for new volunteers; Completed and Canceled are locked down.

Scheduling the work

Set the Scheduled Date (and optionally a Completion Date after the project wraps). If the project is tied to a serve day event, picking an Associated Event auto-fills the scheduled date from the event record.

Skills and equipment needed

Fill in Skills Needed and Equipment Needed as free-text. These appear on the public Upcoming Projects list so volunteers can self-select into projects that match what they can offer.

Family information

The Family Information panel pulls from the families datastore via an integration. You can't edit family details here — if something needs to change, flag SWAT Leadership. Check Family Communication Complete once you've confirmed scope and timing with the family.

Captain and hours

SWAT Leadership can reassign the Project Captain from this page (the dropdown is read-only for captains who aren't leadership). Total Project Man Hours is auto-summed from Tasks; you can override it on the Details page if volunteers logged hours differently from the task plan.

Field reference

Project Status
<em>Planning</em>, <em>Ready to Work</em>, <em>Active</em>, <em>Ongoing</em>, <em>Completed</em>, <em>Canceled</em>. Controls public visibility and the project banner.
Scheduled Date
When the work will happen. Auto-fills from Associated Event if selected.
Completion Date
When the project was finished. Leave empty until the project closes out.
Skills Needed
Free-text description of the skills you're recruiting for.
Equipment Needed
Free-text list of tools/materials volunteers should bring or have available.
Associated Event
Optional — links this project to a record in the <code>events</code> form so the project shows up for that serve day.
Family Type
<em>Foster Family</em>, <em>Special Needs Family</em>, <em>Single Parent Family</em>, <em>Senior Citizen</em>, <em>Other</em>. Multi-select. Used for reporting.
Family Communication Complete
Checkbox — confirms you've reached the family and scope is agreed.
Tips
  • The status banner at the top of every project page links straight back to the Details tab so you can fix the root cause without hunting.
  • If the Associated Event dropdown looks empty, confirm the event is in <em>Open</em> or <em>Planning</em> status — closed events are filtered out.
  • Only SWAT Leadership can change the Project Captain. Don't try to reassign a project to yourself from the volunteer side — ask Leadership.

Managing Volunteers

The Volunteers tab lists everyone currently signed up for your project and lets you add more, capture day-of attendance, and reach the team by text or email. Records live in the <code>swat-project-volunteers</code> datastore.

How to get there

Open a project, then click Volunteers in the sidebar.

Adding a volunteer

Type a first or last name into the typeahead — it searches the volunteers datastore and shows matches as you type. Select someone and the portal creates a swat-project-volunteers record with Status: Active. If the volunteer is already on the project, they won't show up in the picker.

Setting how many additional volunteers you need

The Additional Volunteers Needed field on the project tells other volunteers and SWAT Leadership how many more helpers you're recruiting. Adjust this as people sign up — when it hits zero, the project stops appearing on the Upcoming Projects list as a need.

Messaging the team

Use the phone and email icons at the top of the volunteer list to open a group SMS or email in your device's default app. The SMS link respects iOS/macOS conventions so it works correctly on every platform.

Marking attendance

On serve day, click the Present toggle next to each volunteer as they arrive. This flags the record so man-hour reporting reflects who actually showed up, not just who signed up.

Removing a volunteer

Click the trash icon to remove a volunteer — this soft-deletes the relationship by setting Status: Removed on the swat-project-volunteers record. The history stays so SWAT Leadership can see who was originally signed up.

Opening volunteer details

Click a volunteer's name to open the Volunteer Detail modal. You'll see their skills, tools, dietary restrictions, photo consent, and contact info — useful for planning lunch or knowing who can operate specific equipment.

Field reference

Status
<em>Active</em> or <em>Removed</em>. Changing status to Removed is a soft-delete; the record stays for history.
Present
True/false — whether the volunteer showed up on serve day. Use this to keep man-hour counts honest.
Volunteer ID
Business identifier of the linked volunteer (preferred over the raw submission ID).
Dietary Restrictions
Shown on the detail modal so you can plan meals appropriately.
Photo Consent
Check before including the volunteer in any photos uploaded under <strong>Photos</strong>.
Additional Volunteers Needed
Lives on the <code>swat-projects</code> record but surfaced here — drives recruitment visibility.
Tips
  • Don't delete a <code>swat-project-volunteers</code> record outright — always use the trash icon in the UI so the status flips to <em>Removed</em> rather than the record being destroyed.
  • The typeahead searches first and last name separately and merges results. If you can't find someone, they may not exist in the <code>volunteers</code> datastore yet — direct them to the public sign-up at <strong>/public/events</strong> or ask SWAT Leadership to create the record.
  • Photo Consent is a <em>Yes/No</em>. Assume <em>No</em> until verified — when in doubt, ask before posting.

Tasks

The Tasks tab is a simple checklist attached to your project — a way to plan the work, estimate man hours, and track what's done. Tasks are stored as JSON on the project submission (<code>Tasks JSON</code> field), so there's no separate form to learn.

How to get there

Open a project, then click Tasks in the sidebar.

Adding a task

Type a task description into the Add a task… input and press Enter (or click Add). New tasks appear at the bottom of the checklist with Done: unchecked and no hours estimate.

Estimating man hours

Enter a number in the hours field next to each task. The total at the bottom sums every task's estimate and is stored as Project Tasks Man Hours Total when you save — it feeds into the SWAT Reports dashboard.

Checking tasks off

Click the checkbox next to a task to toggle done. Done tasks stay in the list (don't disappear) so you can see progress at a glance. Unchecking and re-checking is fine — the order is preserved.

Removing a task

Click the trash icon to remove a task from the list. Unlike volunteers, tasks are not soft-deleted — once removed, they're gone. You'll see a save prompt before the change commits.

Saving changes

Any edits (add, remove, toggle, hours) are queued until you click Save. The button only activates when there are unsaved changes. A toast confirms the save and refreshes the project.

Field reference

text
Description of the task. Free-text, no length limit.
done
Checkbox — true if the task is complete.
estimatedHours
Number of man hours this task is expected to take. Blank or non-numeric values count as zero.
Project Tasks Man Hours Total
Field on the <code>swat-projects</code> record — auto-summed from task estimates on save.
Tips
  • Hours are for <em>estimation and reporting</em>, not time tracking. SWAT Leadership uses the sum to track impact across the organization.
  • If you leave the page with unsaved changes, your work isn't lost on the current tab but won't persist across reloads. Hit <strong>Save</strong> before switching projects.
  • There's no built-in per-volunteer assignment today — use task text like "(Joe & Sarah) Install shelves" if you need to note who's doing what.

Expenses & Reimbursements

Coming Soon

Submit and track reimbursements for project expenses — available in an upcoming release. For now, document reimbursement requests in the project <strong>Notes</strong> tab and coordinate with SWAT Leadership directly.

Photos & Notes

Two separate tabs cover documentation. <strong>Photos</strong> uploads image attachments onto the project record. <strong>Notes</strong> captures prose and planning detail with full version history stored in the <code>project-notes</code> datastore.

How to get there

Open a project, then click Photos or Notes in the sidebar.

Uploading photos

On the Photos tab, drag-and-drop image files or click Upload to pick from your device. Files are saved onto the swat-projects submission in the Project Photos field as multipart attachments.

Viewing and removing photos

Uploaded images render as a gallery grid. Click any image to open the full-size view in a new tab. Use the trash icon on a thumbnail to remove an attachment from the project record.

Writing a note

On the Notes tab, the editor at the top is a rich-text field (Toast UI Editor) that supports markdown. Type your note and click Save — each save creates a new project-notes submission tied to the project.

Reading the history

Below the editor is a chronological list of every version, newest first. Each entry shows the author, timestamp, and a word-level diff against the previous version — added words highlight green, removed words strike through in red. Expand any entry to see the full content.

Legacy notes

Older projects may have a single Project Notes field (legacy) instead of the version-controlled list. If you see a plain text area on load, any new saves will migrate to the new project-notes datastore going forward.

Field reference

Project Photos
Multipart attachment field on <code>swat-projects</code>. Accepts images; anything else works but won't render inline.
Content (project-notes)
Rich-text/markdown field on each note version. Rendered as-is in the history view.
Project ID (project-notes)
Links the note to its parent project. Don't edit directly.
Project Notes (legacy)
Deprecated single-field free-text on the project itself. Read-only for new writes.
Tips
  • Ask volunteers for <em>Photo Consent: Yes</em> before uploading any photos that include them (see the Volunteers tab detail modal).
  • Notes are append-only — you can't edit a previous version. If you need to correct something, write a new note referencing the fix.
  • The word-diff is useful for quick review, but it can look noisy on formatting changes. Expand to see the full content when in doubt.

Requesting Help & Event Association

Two levers help you pull in more volunteers: setting the <strong>Additional Volunteers Needed</strong> count so your project surfaces on the public Upcoming Projects list, and linking an <strong>Associated Event</strong> so signups from a serve day can be routed to your project.

How to get there

Both controls live on the Details tab of your project.

Broadcasting a need on Upcoming Projects

Set Additional Volunteers Needed on Details to a number greater than zero, and set status to Ready to Work. The project will then appear on the public Upcoming Projects listing (/upcoming-projects) with its skills/equipment needs, and any logged-in volunteer can request to join via the request-to-join-swat-project form.

Associating with a serve day event

On Details, pick an event in the Associated Event dropdown (only Open and Planning events appear). This sets the project's Scheduled Date from the event and lets SWAT Leadership route event signups into your project on the Assign page.

How assignments flow in

When SWAT Leadership assigns a signed-up volunteer to your project from the Event Assign page, a new swat-project-volunteers record is created automatically — the volunteer will appear on your Volunteers tab within moments.

Joining a project directly

If a volunteer in your network can't find your project on Upcoming Projects (maybe it's set to Planning), they can still join via a direct link you share: /kapps/service-portal/forms/request-to-join-swat-project?Project%20ID=<yourProjectID>. The request creates a task for Leadership (not you) so they can vet the match.

Field reference

Additional Volunteers Needed
Integer on <code>swat-projects</code>. When > 0 and status is <em>Ready to Work</em>, the project is listed publicly for recruitment.
Associated Event
Submission ID of a record in the <code>events</code> form. Filters to <em>Open</em> and <em>Planning</em> status in the dropdown.
Skills Needed / Equipment Needed
The two fields surfaced on Upcoming Projects listings — make these specific so the right volunteers self-select.
Tips
  • Lower Additional Volunteers Needed as people sign up — a project showing "need 10 more" when you have enough confuses recruitment.
  • If the Associated Event dropdown is empty, the event may have been closed out. Ask SWAT Leadership to reopen it (or link a different event).
  • Direct-link join requests still route through SWAT Leadership. You don't have to approve anyone yourself — Leadership fills that role.
Waterboyz · https://waterboyz.kinops.io
